structure.affirmative: Subject + will be + V-ing — I will be working. / She will be sleeping.
structure.negative: Subject + won't be + V-ing — I won't be working. / She won't be sleeping.
structure.question: Will + Subject + be + V-ing? — Will you be working? / Will she be sleeping?

usage.text: Action in progress at a specific future moment

faq.q: What's the difference between Future Simple and Future Continuous?
faq.a: 'Will work' = a decision or prediction (the action as a whole). 'Will be working' = the action in progress at a specific future moment: 'At noon I will be working' means you are already mid-task at noon.

faq.q: Can I use Future Continuous for polite questions?
faq.a: Yes — 'Will you be using the printer?' sounds softer than 'Will you use the printer?' It implies asking about existing plans rather than making a demand.

faq.q: Does Future Continuous work with stative verbs?
faq.a: No. Stative verbs (know, love, belong, seem) don't have continuous forms. Use Future Simple: 'I will know the answer', not 'I will be knowing'.
